attrs.alt: Microsoft Entra Permissions Management CIEM architecture

content.text: Cloud Infrastructure Entitlement Management (CIEM) addresses the identity-to-permission gap in multi-cloud environments: the fact that cloud identities — users, service principals, workload identities, and serverless functions — are almost always granted far more permissions than they ever use. Microsoft Entra Permissions Management (EPM) is Microsoft's CIEM solution, providing continuous discovery, analysis, and right-sizing of permissions across Azure, AWS, and Google Cloud Platform from a single control plane.

Studies consistently find that 90%+ of granted cloud permissions are never used. A single over-privileged identity that is compromised can therefore access vastly more resources than it needs. CIEM quantifies and eliminates this risk.
Enumerate every identity and all permissions granted across all onboarded clouds
Unified inventory — human users, SPs, roles, policies, resource permissions
Score (0-100) measuring gap between granted and actually-used permissions
Recommend and generate least-privilege policies based on actual usage (90-day window)
Custom IAM policies / Azure role definitions with only used actions
Just-in-time elevated access for a specific task and time window
Temporary policy created and automatically revoked after expiry
Real-time alerts when identities perform actions outside their normal pattern
Activity alerts forwarded to Entra audit logs and Sentinel
Usage, entitlements, and PCI reports per cloud and per identity

The Permission Creep Index is the central metric in EPM. It quantifies how much of a cloud identity's granted permissions went unused in the last 90 days. The score is normalised to 0-100 and weighted by identity type and sensitivity.

# Permission Creep Index (PCI) — conceptual calculation
# PCI measures the gap between permissions granted and permissions actually used

# For each identity:
#   granted_actions  = total number of unique cloud API actions the identity CAN perform
#   used_actions     = unique actions the identity DID perform in the last 90 days
#   unused_actions   = granted_actions - used_actions
#   raw_pci          = unused_actions / granted_actions  (0.0 to 1.0)

# PCI is then normalised to 0-100 and weighted by identity type:
#   - Human identities (users) have higher weight than service principals
#   - Inactive identities (no usage in 90 days) get maximum PCI (100)
#   - Super-identities (can perform >2000 actions) are flagged separately

# Tenant PCI = weighted average across all discovered identities
# A PCI of 0 means every identity uses exactly the permissions it has been granted
# A PCI of 100 means all identities have never used any granted permission

# EPM dashboard shows PCI per cloud (AWS / Azure / GCP) and per identity

EPM onboards each cloud account as an Authorization System. For Azure, it needs Reader and User Access Administrator roles to enumerate subscriptions. For AWS, it needs a cross-account IAM role. For GCP, it needs a service account with Viewer roles.

# Step 1: Enable Entra Permissions Management for the tenant
# Done via Entra portal -> Permissions Management -> Get Started
# Requires Global Administrator role

# Step 2: Onboard an Azure subscription
# EPM needs read access to enumerate role assignments and audit logs

# Create a service principal for EPM data collection
Connect-AzAccount
$sp = New-AzADServicePrincipal -DisplayName 'EntraPermissionsManagement'

# Assign Reader role at subscription scope
New-AzRoleAssignment `
  -ObjectId $sp.Id `
  -RoleDefinitionName 'Reader' `
  -Scope "/subscriptions/$((Get-AzContext).Subscription.Id)"

# Assign 'User Access Administrator' at subscription scope (needed to read role assignments)
New-AzRoleAssignment `
  -ObjectId $sp.Id `
  -RoleDefinitionName 'User Access Administrator' `
  -Scope "/subscriptions/$((Get-AzContext).Subscription.Id)"

# Step 3: Enter the SP credentials in the EPM portal to complete onboarding
# EPM then begins a 24-hour initial discovery scan
Reading EPM Data via Microsoft Graph
EPM exposes its authorization system inventory and PCI data through the Microsoft Graph beta endpoint under /external/authorizationSystems.

# Read EPM findings via Microsoft Graph (beta endpoint)
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'PermissionsManagement.Read.All'

# List all onboarded authorization systems (clouds)
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET `
  -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/external/authorizationSystems' |
  Select-Object -ExpandProperty value |
  Select-Object id, displayName, authorizationSystemType

# Get the Permission Creep Index for each authorization system
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET `
  -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/external/authorizationSystems' |
  Select-Object -ExpandProperty value | ForEach-Object {
      $sysId = $_.id
      $pci = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET `
        -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/external/authorizationSystems/$sysId/microsoft.graph.azureAuthorizationSystem/permissionsCreepIndexDistributions"
      Write-Host "$($_.displayName): PCI distribution - $($pci.value)"
  }

POD replaces standing privileged access with time-bound, task-scoped permissions. An identity requests the specific actions it needs for a task; an approver grants them for a defined window; EPM automatically revokes the permission when the window closes.

EPM identifies identities with a high PCI score and generates custom role/policy definitions containing only the actions that identity actually used. Deploy the custom role and remove the over-privileged assignment.

# Right-size an over-privileged Azure role assignment using EPM recommendations
# EPM generates a 'Permissions on Demand' report showing unused actions per identity

# Step 1: Get the unused permissions report for a specific identity
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET `
  -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/external/authorizationSystems/<sys-id>/microsoft.graph.azureAuthorizationSystem/associatedIdentities/users" |
  Select-Object -ExpandProperty value |
  Where-Object { $_.permissionsCreepIndex.score -gt 67 } |  # High PCI
  Select-Object displayName, userPrincipalName, @{N='PCI';E={$_.permissionsCreepIndex.score}}

# Step 2: Generate a least-privilege custom role from EPM's recommendation
# EPM outputs a JSON role definition containing only used actions
# Deploy the custom role and remove the over-privileged built-in assignment:

$customRole = @{
    Name        = 'Least-Privilege-StorageContributor'
    Description = 'Auto-generated by EPM right-sizing — only used Storage actions'
    Actions     = @('Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/read', 'Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices/containers/read')
    AssignableScopes = @('/subscriptions/<sub-id>')
} 
New-AzRoleDefinition -Role ([Microsoft.Azure.Commands.Resources.Models.Authorization.PSRoleDefinition]$customRole)
EPM writes audit events to Entra Audit Logs when it grants or revokes permissions. Monitor these events in Log Analytics to track all EPM-driven permission changes.

// EPM generates audit events in Entra Audit Logs when permissions are granted or revoked
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where LoggedByService == 'Permissions Management'
| extend Actor  = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName ?? InitiatedBy.app.displayName)
| extend Action = tostring(OperationName)
| extend Target = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| project TimeGenerated, Actor, Action, Target, Result
| order by TimeGenerated desc
RBAC roles, custom roles, resource policies
IAM users, roles, groups, policies, SCPs
Workload Identity Federation or service account key
Azure Activity Log + Entra Audit Logs
Microsoft Entra Permissions Management (standalone SKU or part of Entra ID Governance)
Global Administrator to enable; Reader + User Access Administrator for data collection SP
Cross-account IAM role with ReadOnlyAccess + CloudTrail access
Service account with Viewer roles on all projects + Cloud Asset Viewer
24 hours for first full scan; incremental updates every few hours after

EPM can alert when an identity performs a cloud action it has never used before, uses a Delete action for the first time, or exceeds a usage threshold. Alerts can be sent via email or forwarded to Sentinel.

The EPM Sentinel data connector streams permission alerts as SecurityAlert events. This enables correlation of EPM findings with sign-in risk, CA policy violations, and other identity signals in a unified incident.

# Connect Entra Permissions Management to Microsoft Sentinel
# Step 1: In Sentinel workspace -> Data connectors -> search 'Microsoft Entra Permissions Management'
# Step 2: Enable the connector — it streams EPM alerts into Sentinel as SecurityAlert events

# KQL: Query EPM alerts in Sentinel
SecurityAlert
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where ProductName == 'Microsoft Entra Permissions Management'
| extend AlertSeverity = AlertSeverity
| extend AffectedIdentity = tostring(Entities[0].Name)
| extend CloudProvider   = tostring(ExtendedProperties.AuthorizationSystem)
| project TimeGenerated, AlertName, AlertSeverity, AffectedIdentity, CloudProvider, Description
| order by TimeGenerated desc

Identities with a PCI score of 100 have had zero cloud activity in 90 days. These are the highest-priority targets for access removal — they represent standing permissions with no operational justification.

# Find inactive identities (no cloud activity in 90 days) via EPM Graph API
# These are prime candidates for deletion or access removal

Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'PermissionsManagement.Read.All'

# Get all Azure identities with PCI score of 100 (completely unused permissions)
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET `
  -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/external/authorizationSystems/<azure-sys-id>/microsoft.graph.azureAuthorizationSystem/associatedIdentities/users?$filter=permissionsCreepIndex/score eq 100' |
  Select-Object -ExpandProperty value |
  Select-Object displayName, userPrincipalName, @{N='PCI';E={$_.permissionsCreepIndex.score}},
    @{N='LastActive';E={$_.lastActiveDateTime}}

# Export to CSV for review
... | Export-Csv inactive-identities.csv -NoTypeInformation

Before deleting an inactive identity's permissions, verify with the owner that the account is not used for break-glass or emergency access. Tag break-glass accounts explicitly and exclude them from automated right-sizing.
Azure RBAC and Entra ID roles only
Azure RBAC + AWS IAM + GCP IAM
All granted AND used permissions across all clouds
Yes — generates least-privilege policies from usage
CIEM addresses the permission creep problem in multi-cloud environments: most cloud identities hold far more permissions than they ever use, creating massive blast radius if compromised.
Microsoft Entra Permissions Management provides CIEM for Azure, AWS, and GCP from a single control plane, calculating the Permission Creep Index (0-100) for every identity.
The three core actions are: Discover (enumerate all permissions), Remediate (right-size to least-privilege), and Monitor (alert on anomalous usage).
Permission on Demand replaces standing privileged access with time-bound, task-scoped grants — EPM creates and automatically revokes the temporary permission.
Right-sizing generates custom role/policy JSON containing only the actions an identity actually used in the last 90 days; deploy it and remove the over-privileged assignment.
EPM writes audit events to Entra Audit Logs and integrates with Microsoft Sentinel for SIEM-level alerting on privilege escalation and anomalous cloud API usage.
